findings.title: No CIFAR-10 / MVTec image-AD code; Table 2 and Table 4 unreproducible
findings.quote:
def main(args):
    setup_seed(42)
    device = torch.device('cuda:0' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu')
    #data_loader
    train_x, train_y, test_x, test_y=loadData(args.datasetname)
    train_dataset=torch.utils.data.TensorDataset(torch.tensor(train_x))
    train_loader = DataLoader(train_dataset, batch_size=args.batch_size)
    test_dataset=torch.utils.data.TensorDataset(torch.tensor(test_x), torch.tensor(test_y))
    test_loader = DataLoader(test_dataset, batch_size=args.batch_size)
    input_dim=torch.tensor(train_x).shape[1]

    #model
    model=UniINN(input_dim=input_dim).to(device)
findings.claim: The only entrypoint loads tabular .mat features and a plain MLP encoder; nothing in the repo loads CIFAR-10, uses the DO2HSC CNN, loads MVTec AD, uses a frozen WideResNet-50 backbone, or computes pixel-level AUROC.
findings.concern: Table 2 (CIFAR-10 + MVTec image/pixel AUROC) and Table 4 (image ablation) — the entire visual-AD half of the paper, including the headline MVTec 99.5/98.3 — have no code that produces them.
findings.resolution: Authors: please add the image-domain training/evaluation code (CIFAR-10 + MVTec, backbones, pixel-AUROC computation) used for Tables 2 and 4.

findings.title: Only 4 of 20 tabular datasets bundled; fetch script covers 13 with mismatched paths
findings.quote:
#breastw
mkdir -p data/breastw
curl -o data/breastw/breastw.mat https://www.dropbox.com/s/g3hlnucj71kfvq4/breastw.mat?dl=1

#cardio
mkdir -p data/cardio
curl -o data/cardio/cardio.mat https://www.dropbox.com/s/galg3ihvxklf0qi/cardio.mat?dl=1


#glass
mkdir -p data/glass
curl -o data/glass/glass.mat https://www.dropbox.com/s/iq3hjxw77gpbl7u/glass.mat?dl=1

#ionosphere
mkdir -p data/ionosphere
curl -o data/ionosphere/ionosphere.mat https://www.dropbox.com/s/lpn4z73fico4uup/ionosphere.mat?dl=1
findings.claim: Table 1 reports 20 tabular datasets, but only glass/thyroid/wbc/wine .mat files are present (see _audit_code/out/repo_completeness.json); get_data.sh fetches only 13, and it downloads into per-dataset subdirs (data/<name>/<name>.mat) whereas loadData.py reads a flat path data/<name>.mat, so even fetched files would not be found.
findings.concern: 16 of the 20 Table-1 rows cannot be reproduced from the repo; the fetch script is both incomplete (13<20) and points to a directory layout the loader does not use.
findings.resolution: Authors: provide all 20 datasets (or working download links) and align get_data.sh output paths with the path loadData.py reads.

findings.title: No ablation/hyperparameter-sweep code for Tables 3 and 5
findings.quote:
for epoch in range(args.num_epoch):
    model.train()
    loss_list = []
    for i, x_train in enumerate(train_loader):
        x_raw=x_train[0].to(device).float()   
        x1,x2,prior,post=model(x_raw)
        loss=reconstruction_loss(x1, x2)+0.5*(prior_loss(prior)+post_loss(post))
        optimizer.zero_grad()
        loss.backward()
        optimizer.step()
        loss_list.append(loss.item())
findings.claim: The single training loop always uses the full RE+DE loss with K/R fixed in model construction (denoise_net(latent_dim, 3)); there is no flag or harness to drop the RE or DE component (Table 3) or to sweep K and R (Table 5).
findings.concern: Table 3 (RE/DE component ablation, incl. the headline '+11.2/4.67 vs baseline' gain) and Table 5 (K×R sweep) cannot be reproduced — the code that produces those rows is absent.
findings.resolution: Authors: provide the ablation driver(s) that toggle RE/DE and sweep K, R.

findings.title: loadData reads a hardcoded absolute path on the authors' machine
findings.claim: The data loader always reads from the absolute path /home/qxl/ddpm/UniINN/data/<name>.mat regardless of where the repo is checked out, ignoring the bundled Unifying_INN/data/ directory and the --save_folder/cwd.
findings.concern: Out of the box the pipeline crashes with FileNotFoundError for every user, so none of the tabular results (Table 1) are reproducible without editing the source.
findings.resolution: Authors: replace the hardcoded path with a relative path (e.g. Unifying_INN/data/) or a configurable --data_dir argument.

findings.title: Typo 'deterministirc' makes cuDNN-determinism flag a silent no-op
findings.claim: The misspelled attribute 'deterministirc' creates a new unused attribute on torch.backends.cudnn; the real flag torch.backends.cudnn.deterministic is never set, so cuDNN remains nondeterministic.
findings.concern: Combined with dropout (Encoder, p=0.2) and GPU nondeterminism, reported single-number AUROC/AP values may not be exactly reproducible; impact is small for the conclusions.
findings.resolution: Authors: fix the typo to torch.backends.cudnn.deterministic = True.

findings.title: No validation set; test AUROC evaluated and printed every 5 epochs during training
findings.quote:
if (epoch+1) % 5 == 0:
    print('epoch: {} loss: {}'.format(epoch+1, avg_loss))
    torch.save(model.state_dict(), os.path.join(args.save_folder, args.datasetname+'.pth'))
    evaluation_batch(model, test_loader,device)
findings.claim: There is no validation split (loadData returns only train and test); every 5 epochs the checkpoint is overwritten and evaluation_batch runs on the test set and prints AUROC/AP, so the test metric is monitored throughout training and a user reads the best/desired printed value.
findings.concern: With the test set as the only signal observed during training and no held-out validation set, epoch/checkpoint selection (and the reported single number) can be biased toward the test set; the paper reports one AUROC/AP per dataset without specifying which epoch is selected.
findings.resolution: Authors: confirm whether the reported numbers are at a fixed final epoch (independent of the printed test AUROC) or selected using the test metric; if the latter, add a held-out validation set for model/epoch selection.
